title: QUIC Connection Runtime High-Density Execution Verification
verification_method: Execution and inspection evidence from focused unit tests.
expected_result: The sharded runtime host keeps a stable handle-to-shard mapping, rejects duplicate ownership, and routes a single connection's events through one owner path even under concurrent posting.
procedure: Run the requirement-home tests for owner exclusivity, centralized transition ownership, effect-returning transitions, stable shard routing, stable handles across route churn, connection-owned ordinary state, single-connection shard ownership, no per-connection runtime consumers under the high-density host, configurable shard count, concurrent event posting, and bounded concurrent-collection usage.
Run the existing CRT inbox tests that prove the one-consumer/many-producer queue pattern used by the shard host.
Confirm the host remains bounded to the configured shard count and that connection ownership stays on one shard for the full sequence.
scope: This verification slice covers the endpoint-side shard host and stable routing seam defined by SPEC-QUIC-CRT. It proves that one connection handle always maps to the same shard, that duplicate ownership is rejected, and that a connection's events stay on one owner path under concurrent posting. It does not attempt to prove timer migration or other downstream CRT slices.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-04-30. The focused CRT high-density execution filter passed 18/18, covering one owner per runtime, centralized transition ownership, effect-returning transitions, stable shard routing, stable handles across route-key churn, endpoint-scoped concurrency, one-consumer/many-producer inbox behavior, no per-connection runtime consumer tasks in the high-density host, configurable shard count, concurrent event posting, and ordinary connection-owned hot-path state. Timer migration and endpoint ingress have separate verification owners.
preconditions: The CRT requirement traits are discoverable by the test runner.
The sharded runtime host and its route registry remain internal to the repository.
